mnemonics.title: CNN Layer Order
mnemonics.content: Remember: C-P-C-P-FC-FC (Conv-Pool-Conv-Pool-Fully Connected-Fully Connected) - Like LeCun's LeNet flow.

mnemonics.title: AlexNet Innovations
mnemonics.content: A.R.D.G. - Activation (ReLU), Regularization (Dropout), Data Augmentation, GPU Training.

mnemonics.title: ReLU Benefits
mnemonics.content: FAST: Fast to compute, Avoids vanishing gradient, Sparse activation, Training is faster.

mnemonics.title: Pooling Types
mnemonics.content: MAX preserves edges, AVG smooths features - MAX is better for distinct features.

acronyms.usage: Deep learning architecture for image recognition
Normalization technique in AlexNet for generalization
Layer where all neurons connect to all neurons in next layer
Network without fully connected layers for pixel-wise prediction

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a flashlight (kernel) sliding over a photo, computing dot products at each position - creating a new transformed image.
Layer 1 = edges (lines), Layer 2 = parts (eyes, nose), Layer 3 = objects (faces) - building from simple to complex.
Like a light switch - if input is positive, it passes through unchanged; if negative, it's cut off (becomes zero).
Taking the loudest voice in a group - preserves the strongest signal while reducing group size.

analogies.analogy: Like a detective scanning a crime scene with a magnifying glass - each position gets examined for specific evidence (features).
Like summarizing a long article - you lose some details but capture the main points in a shorter form.
Like different colored filters on a camera - each filter highlights different aspects of the same scene.
Like a team where each member must learn to do their job independently because teammates might be absent any day.
Like someone who learned to ride a bicycle and then uses that balance skill to learn motorcycle riding faster.
